question: Will turnout in the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff be between 600,000 and 900,000 voters?
description:
The Texas Senate Republican primary runoff election is scheduled for May 26, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the total vote count in the Texas Republican Senate Primary runoff election (the race’s total canvass votes).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the results of the election aren’t known by November 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.  

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the State of Texas, such as official statewide results published by the Texas Secretary of State (https://www.sos.state.tx.us/index.shtml); however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dismal early voting turnout in the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff, totaling around 135,000 ballots through May 24, anchors trader consensus toward the 0.6–0.9 million total vote range at 40% implied probability, well below 2022's 627,000 runoff figure. Lower enthusiasm stems from the absence of marquee statewide races, limited candidate mobilization, and forecasts of hot Election Day weather on May 28. Higher buckets like 1.2–1.5 million (26.5%) reflect optimism from GOP voter outreach efforts, but subdued participation echoes off-year runoff patterns, with markets pricing in under 1 million overall amid steady pre-election quiet.

question: Will Mayes Middleton win the Texas Republican primary runoff for Attorney General?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Texas Republican Primary runoff election for state Attorney General, scheduled for May 26, 2026.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Texas Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Texas Attorney General Republican Primary Runoff Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Texas Republican Primary runoff election for state Attorney General, scheduled for May 26, 2026.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Texas Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors State Rep. Mayes Middleton at 72.5% implied probability to win a potential Republican primary runoff for Texas Attorney General, driven by his aggressive fundraising—over $1 million raised early—and endorsements from key conservative figures like Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick allies, positioning him as a strong anti-incumbent challenger to Ken Paxton. U.S. Rep. Chip Roy trails at 38%, buoyed by national profile and district-level popularity but tempered by his public focus on congressional reelection rather than a statewide bid. Recent catalysts include Paxton's ongoing legal battles and ethics probes eroding his frontrunner status in early polls, alongside Middleton's district wins signaling grassroots momentum; traders eye 2026 filing deadlines and convention endorsements as next market movers.

question: Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa out as leader of Bahrain?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the King of Bahrain, Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, ceases to be the de facto leader of Bahrain at any point between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa will be considered to no longer be the de facto leader of Bahrain if he is removed from power, is detained, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from acting as the de facto leader of Bahrain within this market's timeframe.

An announcement of  Khalifa's resignation or abdication will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of when the announced departure goes into effect. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa remaining Bahrain's leader, with "No" at 89.5%, driven by his ongoing public role and absence of official announcements on health issues, abdication, or instability. The 74-year-old monarch, reigning since 1999, recently hosted diplomatic engagements and national events, signaling continuity in the constitutional monarchy. Crown Prince Salman bin Hamad Al Khalifa's role as prime minister since 2020 bolsters governance stability amid Bahrain's Gulf alliances. No verified reports of protests, coups, or succession moves from royal sources justify the low odds of change, with traders awaiting any court statements.

question: Will gas hit (High) $5.00 by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if on any day between market creation and March 31, 2026, the average US regular gas price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the first two digits of the reported price (e.g., if the price is reported as $3.157, this market will resolve to the "$3.15" bracket).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the American Automotive Association (AAA), presently found here: https://gasprices.aaa.com/. Specifically, the cell under "Regular" and for the row "Current Avg.".

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 28% implied probability to U.S. national average gasoline prices hitting $3.50 per gallon by March 31, reflecting ample inventories and softening crude oil dynamics as the primary drivers. Current AAA-reported average stands at $3.23/gallon, down 5% from January peaks, amid WTI crude hovering near $81/barrel—pressured by China demand slowdowns and elevated U.S. refinery utilization at 92%. EIA data shows gasoline stocks 3% above five-year averages, capping upside. Key catalysts ahead include Thursday's EIA Petroleum Status Report and OPEC+ production talks, with recession fears from upcoming Fed projections potentially weighing further on energy demand.

question: Will Kevin Warsh be confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next individual formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve.

Formal confirmation as Chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm a nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Kevin Warsh at 94.2% implied probability for Senate confirmation as next Federal Reserve Chair, driven by multiple reports from credible outlets citing President-elect Trump's strong preference for the former Fed governor, whose hawkish monetary policy views and prior service from 2006-2011 align with calls for tighter control over the central bank. question: Starmer out by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Keir Starmer ceases to be the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for any period of time between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Keir Starmer's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of the UK, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Keir Starmer tenure markets reflects heightened skepticism fueled by Labour's post-budget poll collapse, with support dipping below 30% in recent MRPs amid public fury over tax hikes on employers and pensioner winter fuel cuts. Chancellor Rachel Reeves' October 30 fiscal statement triggered farmer protests, MP rebellions on welfare bills, and Reform UK's surge to 20%+. Starmer quelled internal threats by reshuffling cabinet and winning conference leadership ballot, though low turnout underscored fragility. Upcoming local elections in May 2025 and potential by-elections loom as catalysts; sustained discontent could prompt no-confidence moves or leadership bids.

question: Will PH win the second most seats in the 2026 Colombian Chamber of Representatives election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the second-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

question: Will PLC win the second most seats in the 2026 Colombian Chamber of Representatives election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the second-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors the Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) at 50.6% implied probability for third place in seat totals from Colombia's upcoming Chamber of Representatives election, with Centro Democrático (CD) at 24.5%, reflecting traditional parties' resurgence amid President Petro's Historic Pact struggles. Recent national polls from Invamer and Datexco show PLC polling around 24-28% nationally, edging CD's 20-25%, while Pacto Histórico slips to 15-18% due to government scandals and 30% approval ratings. Key drivers include PLC's organizational strength and CD's loyal Uribista base, bolstered by strong showings in October 2023 regional contests. Upcoming party conventions could shift dynamics, but current pricing highlights skepticism on leftist consolidation for top-three finishes.

Upsets remain possible given international unpredictability.

The visitors advanced to the Euro 2024 round of 16 with wins over Portugal and Denmark, boasting stars like RB Leipzig striker Benjamin Sesko and Atletico Madrid keeper Jan Oblak, while remaining unbeaten in World Cup qualifiers. Montenegro, hosting at Podgorica, struggles with a three-match losing streak and defensive frailties, though home advantage could spark an upset. No major injuries reported, but Slovenia's rest edge post-internationals tilts implied probabilities, reflecting historical head-to-head dominance.
